target: Lombard Finance cross-layer (Solana lombard_token_pool + EVM GMP core)
primary_target_subsystem: Solana lombard_token_pool release_or_mint/lock_or_burn plus mailbox/bridge/consortium cross-layer handling

validated_tests.rust_units_localnet: all-green across lombard_token_pool, consortium, bridge, mailbox, bascule_gmp, asset_router, lbtc
validated_tests.anchor_ts_aggregate: 149 / 165 passing; 16 fail in before-all hooks from shared-PDA cross-pollution in single validator session
validated_tests.anchor_ts_per_file: 310 passing across 11 TS suites (asset_router 85, bascule.bankrun 12, bascule 1, bascule_gmp 21, bridge 71, ccip 7, consortium 17, consortium_utilities 23, mailbox 54, ratio_oracle 18, registry 1)
validated_tests.crucible_token_pool_dryrun: PASS (1 tracked accounts, 1 program loaded, single iteration completed)

new_signals.summary: release_or_mint_tokens calls mailbox.handle_message with handler=token-pool state PDA; pool_signer is a remaining account for bridge.gmp_receive. Therefore destination_caller must equal state PDA bytes, not pool_signer bytes.

Mailbox marks message_info Handled before recipient CPI. Normal Solana transaction atomicity should roll back this write when bridge.gmp_receive reverts. A validator-backed fixture remains the next executable step.

EVM Mailbox._deliverAndHandle wraps IHandler.handlePayload in try/catch and only sets handledPayload[payloadHash]=true on success; handler revert does NOT bubble and message stays Delivered / re-attemptable. Solana mailbox.handle_message writes Handled then invoke_signed, Anchor transaction atomicity rolls back Handled write on recipient CPI revert. Both gates dedupe via PDA init keyed on [MESSAGE_SEED, payload_hash]. Net effect: Solana requires a fresh tx after a failed gmp_receive; EVM allows re-deliverAndHandle on the same Delivered payload.

Crucible scaffold dry-run for lombard_token_pool is now PASSING after copying the mainnet-feature-built lombard_token_pool.so (686 KB) into the scaffold target/deploy/. Crucible reported: DRY-RUN Harness Validation passed! - 1 tracked accounts - 1 programs loaded - Single iteration completed successfully.
Anchor TS aggregate run showed 16 before-all failures from shared-PDA cross-pollution; per-file isolation (anchor-test-each.sh) confirms these are test-infra, not protocol bugs.

next_steps: Add a single-file Anchor TS integration in tests/lombard_cross_layer_v651.ts that asserts: (a) destination_caller=state PDA succeeds; (b) destination_caller=pool_signer reverts InvalidDestinationCaller; (c) release_or_mint with downstream bridge.gmp_receive failing rolls back mailbox.message_info.status to Delivered; (d) decimal-mismatch release_or_mint fully rolls back recipient balance, rate-limit, message status, and message_handled PDA.

Crucible stateful sequence fuzzing: define action set (init_global_config, initialize_token_pool, init_chain_remote_config, set_remote_chain_rate_limit, deliver_message_mock, release_or_mint_succeed, release_or_mint_revert_due_to_amount_mismatch, release_or_mint_revert_due_to_destination_caller_mismatch) and run at least 3 fresh-context stateful sequences.

Hardhat EVM probe for cross-layer divergence: Mailbox._deliverAndHandle with a revert-throwing handler; confirm handledPayload[payloadHash] stays false and the same payload can be retried via a second deliverAndHandle call.

Hardhat EVM probe: AssetRouter._changeBascule(0) admin disable-mint path; reproduce an admin call that disables the bascule check; quantify whether a single role-default-admin action can suppress mint validation.
Lab notebook addendum at data/security_results/lab_notebook/2026-07-04-lombard-cross-layer-crucible-resolved.md recording round-2 transitions.
